senses.definition: Common or usual; not exceptional.
Lacking special or striking qualities; average or typical.
Indicating origin or source.
Indicating movement out of or from within something.
Indicating material, cause, or means (of, through).
A person's habitual mental and moral qualities or temperament.
The distinguishing qualities or general type of something.
The set of guiding beliefs or habitual ways a person or thing acts.
Having taken place or elapsed (used of a period or event).
That has flowed or passed along (literal use, of liquid or of time as a metaphor).
Having occurred not long ago.
The abstract, measurable continuum in which events occur.
A particular span or era of history or life.
A suitable or particular moment for something to happen.
Neuter demonstrative pronoun used to point to something or introduce a statement.
Neutral pronoun referring to a previously mentioned idea or situation.
A person who represents or stands for a group, class, or idea.
A typical example or member of a type or generation.
Continuing to be the case; remaining so.
An additional amount or degree.
Up to now (often in negatives or questions).
Living on until an advanced age or until the present; remaining alive.
Continuing to live through the remainder of one's life (of a person or cohort).
People born and living around the same time; a cohort sharing age and cultural position.
A particular historical period characterized by shared traits or events.
Demonstrative used to point to a nearby or just-mentioned person or thing.
Used to identify the specific person or thing just mentioned or about to be described.
A person who composes literary works such as books or poems.
A person who invents or fabricates stories; a teller of tales.
Excludes others; no more than a particular thing or person.
Used to downplay importance, meaning ‘simply’ or ‘just’.
Introduces a contrast or restriction, similar to ‘but’.
To have the physical or mental ability to do something.
To express possibility or permission (can/may/might).
Used impersonally to indicate that something can happen or be done.
Connects clauses to express contrast or opposition.
Used to restrict or limit (older or literary use equivalent to ‘only’).
Emphatically includes a surprising or extreme case within a statement.
Used to heighten emphasis or contrast in a sentence.
Proper or expected in the circumstances; fitting or appropriate.
Required by duty, law, or moral necessity; obligatory.
Used predicatively in older/literary style to mean ‘should’ or ‘ought to’.
To have actual being or presence; to be real or alive.
To continue to be or to remain in force or use over time.
Indicates location within something or someone (in, inside, at).
Indicates motion toward the interior or entry (into).
Used to mark time periods or contexts (in, during).
Possessive determiner indicating that something belongs to or concerns us.
A large group of people living together in an ordered community or culture.
A formal group or organization of people united for a purpose.
To take hold of, pick up, or seize something physically.
To assume a position, role, or attitude (to take on something).
To consider or factor something into a decision or judgment.
A thought, reason, or motive taken into account when judging or deciding.
An intelligent thought or opinion formed after reflection.
A practical assessment or calculation of what is expedient or possible.
A demonstrative used to indicate a specific person or thing at some distance from speaker.
Used to single out or identify one particular item or person among others.
Refers to someone or something previously mentioned or known; 'that former'.
A fact or condition that accompanies or influences an event or situation.
A grammatical term for an adverbial modifier (contextual usage in grammar/literary description).
Indicates location at or near someone or something (e.g., 'at the house').
Indicates time under someone's rule or during a certain period (e.g., 'during the reign of...').
Indicates being in someone's presence or under their authority (e.g., 'in front of him').
Genitive plural form of который used to refer back to plural antecedents ('of whom/which').
Accusative plural form used for animate antecedents or as required by the verb/preposition.
Refers to the general case or overall situation rather than specifics.
Used to emphasize negation or totality (colloquial sense: 'not at all').
To come together or develop into a definite form or result (e.g., 'events take shape').
To result or turn out in a certain way (colloquial: 'how things are turning out').
To fold or be folded (physical sense, less common).
First-person singular subject pronoun (‘I’).
The name of the Cyrillic letter Я (used when referring to the letter itself).
Historically or dialectally appears in older forms or variations (rare literary uses).
To desire or wish for something.
Used to express intention or inclination (e.g., 'to want to do something').
To lead or take someone or something out (physically remove).
To introduce, exhibit, or put before an audience or the public.
To deduce or obtain a result (e.g., derive a conclusion or formula).
Indicates spatial position facing or before someone or something.
Indicates being in someone's presence or facing an audience (e.g., 'before the public').
Indicates temporal precedence (e.g., 'before an event').
A group of people who observe, attend, or receive a performance or public event.
The general public or mass of people (can be neutral or pejorative).
Notable or distinguished; recognized as important or influential.
Conspicuous or easily seen; noticeable in appearance.
Connects words or clauses, adding one item to another.
Adds emphasis or unexpected addition (equivalent to "also" or "even").
A person who wrote or created a text or work.
The person responsible for an idea, statement, or plan.
A short written message or reminder.
A brief written communication or informal letter, often conveying information or requests.
Forms the superlative to indicate the highest degree of a quality.
Emphatic determiner meaning those exact or particular items or people.
Expresses that something is obvious or to be expected.
To create something imaginary, such as a story or detail.
To fabricate or devise (often implying falsehood).
Refers back to something just mentioned, often used in comparative constructions ('by that', 'thereby').
Used in phrases to mark degree or contrast (as in 'тем более' = 'all the more').
Negates a verb, adjective, or clause.
Indicates a smaller degree or extent.
Used to compare, meaning 'to a lesser degree' or 'not as...'.
Indicates a quality or type previously mentioned or evident.
Used as a pronoun to refer to a particular kind or person.
The front part of a person's head, used literally.
A human being considered as an actor or subject in events.
An entity, party, or official regarded in a formal or legal role.
Introduces comparison or similarity ('in the way that').
Asks about manner or degree; used in direct or indirect questions.
Older/literary use meaning 'when' or 'after', introducing a temporal clause.
Dative plural form of 'который', used to mean 'to which' or 'to whom'.
Instrumental masculine/neuter singular form of 'который', used to mean 'by which' or 'with which'.
Neuter personal pronoun meaning 'it' or 'that', used for inanimate or abstract subjects.
Used as an anticipatory or expletive subject in impersonal constructions ('it was…', 'it happened that…').
Perfective verb meaning 'to appear' or 'to come into view' or 'to arrive'.
To be revealed or turn out to be (someone/something), often in a formal or literary sense.
Past tense (neuter singular) of 'to be', used to mean 'was'.
Impersonal past form used to indicate existence or occurrence ('there was', 'it happened').
The surrounding conditions, social or intellectual milieu in which something exists or develops.
The day of the week 'Wednesday'.
Meaning 'next' in sequence or 'following' in time or order.
Used as a pronoun or determiner to introduce what comes next ('the following').
Perfective verb meaning 'to come' or 'to arrive' at a place or time.
In literary use, to come to pass; an event or state 'comes' or occurs.
To reach a mental state or realization ('it came to him').
Indicates that something has happened prior to now or earlier than expected.
In negative contexts, means 'no longer' or 'not anymore'.
Meaning authentic, true, or genuine.
Referring to the present time, current state, or contemporary.
Meaning actual, proper, or rightful (e.g., 'the real/proper X').
Preposition meaning 'about', 'concerning', or 'with regard to'.
Indefinite determiner meaning 'some' or 'a certain', indicating an unspecified person or thing.
Used in some contexts to mean 'several' or 'a number of', rather than a single specific item.
An occurrence or incident; something that happens.
A noteworthy or significant occurrence, often public, historical, or of narrative importance.
To have a disease or to be physically unwell.
To experience a localized pain or soreness.
To feel anxious or distressed about something.
To back or root for a person or team (colloquial use).
The anatomical organ that detoxifies and metabolizes substances in the body.
Figuratively, the seat of emotions or courage in older/poetic usage.
Used to introduce a contrast or qualification to what was just said.
Used to add a concluding remark or change the subject; 'in any case'.
A negative particle used to strengthen negation (e.g., 'not even', 'never').
A coordinating particle used in pairs (ни... ни...) meaning 'neither... nor...'.
The seed-bearing cone of a pine or fir tree.
A small hard swelling or protuberance (regional/colloquial).
Slang or figurative: nothing, zero (as in the phrase 'ни шиша').
To have knowledge or understanding of a subject (often used with a negation: 'I don't know much about it').
To perceive or grasp the meaning of something (less common).
A possessive pronoun indicating ownership or close relation to the speaker.
A disorder or condition that impairs normal functioning of the body or mind.
A figurative moral or social ailment; used metaphorically in literary contexts.
To have information, awareness, or acquaintance with facts or persons.
To notice or be conscious of something (to realize).
To have skill or ability to do something ('to know how').
An archaic or colloquial parenthetical used to signal inference or assumption ('one must suppose').
Used to indicate that something is likely or presumed to be true.
Used to express confidence or reassurance about a statement (slightly colloquial).
To receive medical care or therapy in order to cure or manage an illness.
To be the object of treatment or care (including non-medical contexts, e.g., therapeutic measures for addiction).
At no time; not ever (used to negate that something has or will happen).
A short passage taken from a larger written work.
A detached piece or fragment of text or speech.
To give a title or heading to a text, chapter, or work.
A space beneath a floor; a cellar or underground room.
Clandestine, secret, or illegal activity or organization operating covertly.
To advise someone to choose or do something as suitable or good.
To praise or formally support a person or thing as fit or proper.
An intensive form meaning 'the very' (used to emphasize identity or exactness).
The genitive/accusative form used reflexively to mean 'of himself/oneself' or 'himself/itself'.
Reflexive pronoun used as the object referring back to the subject (himself/herself/themselves).
A possessive pronoun meaning 'one's own', agreeing with the possessed noun and usually referring back to the subject.
Used to indicate something belonging to a person or group associated with the subject (our/their own).
A look or direction of the eyes; a visual glance.
A person's way of seeing or thinking about something; an opinion or perspective.
A particle used to form conditional or subjunctive moods (would, would have).
A modal particle used to soften statements, express wishes, or make hypothetical remarks.
To discover or determine information that was unknown; to ascertain.
To make something clear or explain ambiguous facts or reasons.
The reason or factor that brings about an event or state.
A justification or stated motive for an action; an excuse or ground.
Indicates movement or position along, on, or across the surface of something.
Marks source, authority, or conformity (as reported by; in accordance with).
Indicates distribution, rate, or allocation (each, apiece, per).
Marks duration of time or the extent of an activity (for, during, over).
Third-person masculine singular pronoun referring to a male person or to an inanimate masculine noun.
Archaic feminine form оне́ historically used for feminine referents or plural ‘they’ in older language.
Morphological variant starting with n- used after prepositions in some forms (e.g., него vs его).
In some genitive/accusative endings the letter г may be realized phonetically as [v] (phonetic observation).
The state of being alive; biological existence.
A person's lifetime or the period from birth to death.
A manner or style of living; one's habitual life or lifestyle.
Figuratively, the contents or events of a life (one's experiences).
Male given name Fyodor, common in Russian literature.
Surname of the Russian novelist Fyodor Dostoevsky; used as a proper name.
A human being; an individual human.
Used to mean a man or adult male in certain contexts; also 'man' in the sense of humankind.
An individual considered for character, behavior, or social role.
Affected by illness; unwell.
A sick person; someone receiving medical care (nounal use).
Chronically or seriously ill; infirm (can be used figuratively of moral or mental disorder).
Feeling or showing anger; enraged or irate.
Morally bad or malicious; harmful in character or intention.
Ill-natured or mean; disposed to hurt or offend.
Not pleasant to look at; lacking physical attractiveness.
Not attractive in other qualities; dull or uninviting.
To form opinions, have a belief, or hold a thought about something.
To reflect on or ponder something thoughtfully.
To intend or plan to do something (often used with an infinitive).
Conjunction introducing a subordinate clause meaning 'that'.
Interrogative pronoun asking for information: 'what?'.
Used after comparatives to mean 'than'.
Used in exclamations to mean 'what a...' or 'how...'.
Preposition with genitive indicating location: at, by, near, or in the possession of someone.
Used in constructions with a noun in the genitive to express possession (e.g., у меня = I have).
First-person singular pronoun in the accusative: 'me' as direct object.
First-person singular pronoun in the genitive: 'of me' or used to mark possession (e.g., у меня).
To grasp the meaning, sense, or intention of something.
To perceive or become aware of a fact or implication.
To interpret or regard something in a particular way.
Contrastive conjunction used like 'but' or 'whereas' to oppose clauses.
Neutral conjunction linking items or clauses, sometimes with a shift of topic.
To be able to do something, to succeed in accomplishing (often with effort).
To achieve a goal or complete an action (often used with an infinitive).
Dative form used to indicate the indirect object 'to/for you' (plural or polite).
Polite or plural address used when speaking formally or to more than one person.
To make something clear or understandable by describing it in detail.
To give reasons or justification for something; to clarify a cause.
Interrogative pronoun used to ask about a person or people.
Indefinite or relative use meaning 'whomever' or 'any person who'.
Used to emphasize precision or exact identity.
Used to specify or single out something more precisely from what was said.
To harm or cause trouble to someone deliberately (often petty revenge).
To cause annoyance or discomfort to someone.
A particular occurrence or example of something.
A situation or event at a particular time.
An unexpected incident or fortuitous circumstance.
Very good; of high quality or performance.
Used as an approving or emphatic reply meaning 'fine' or 'good'.
In a satisfactory, pleasant, or proper manner; of good quality.
Used to agree, assent, or indicate acceptance.
Not at all; by no means; used to express complete negation.
Used in negative contexts to indicate impossibility or inability (e.g., 'никак не могу' = 'I simply cannot').
Indicates degree — to the extent or degree just mentioned.
Used in comparisons or correlatives (often with «что») to mean 'so... that...'.
Introduces a subordinate clause of purpose or intended result.
Colloquial/short form used to introduce clauses expressing intent, desire, or consequence.
The science and study of diagnosing, treating, and preventing illness.
The system or practice of providing medical treatment and services.
To the degree or amount needed; enough.
Used predicatively to mean 'it is enough' or 'that suffices'.
To create or give shape to something; to constitute or set up.
To make up or compose (a group, whole, or structure).
(Older/literary) To instruct or give someone an education.
To exist or serve as a copula linking subject and predicate.
To take place or happen; used existentially ('there is/are').
A negative response or denial.
Indicates absence or nonexistence when used with a noun in the genitive.
Indicates accompaniment, association, or manner.
Can indicate origin, separation, or starting point in contexts like motion or time ('from', 'since').
Euphonic variant of «с» used before certain consonant clusters or pronouns; same meanings as «с».
A strong feeling of displeasure or rage.
Hostile intent or spiteful disposition toward others.
Draws attention to something present or indicated: 'here is', 'there is'.
Discourse marker used to introduce or emphasize a statement or reaction.
(Polite/archaic) To condescend or be willing (used in polite formulas: 'would you be so kind').
Polite formula used to offer or ask (e.g., 'изволите ли вы...?' = 'would you like...?').
Introduces a conditional clause: on the condition that something happens.
Used to introduce an indirect yes/no question or alternative possibility.
In this manner or way; therefore in consequence or as a result.
Indicates manner or direction: like that / in that fashion.
Used to draw a conclusion or state what follows from a previous remark.
The organ (the liver), usually in informal or familiar speech.
Liver prepared or served as food; a culinary item.
Expresses permission or a directive: allow or cause someone to do something (let it be).
Expresses resignation or acceptance: 'may it be' / 'so be it'.
Possessive pronoun meaning belonging to or associated with a female person or a feminine noun.
Accusative or genitive form used as a direct object or object of a preposition referring to a female person or feminine noun.
Physically powerful or well-built; resisting force or strain.
Durable, well-made, or not easily broken; reliable in construction or character.
Having a strong degree of a quality (e.g., strong tea, sound sleep).
At a distant time in the past.
Indicating a long duration extending up to the present.
To be alive; to exist as a living being.
To have one's home in a place; to dwell or inhabit.
To conduct one's life in a certain way or under certain conditions.
The act or process of flying; movement through the air (archaic or poetic usage).
At the present time; at this moment.
Indicating a change or new state continuing into the future.
Dative form meaning 'to' or 'for' me, used for indirect objects and benefactive constructions.
Used in constructions expressing sensation or emotional states (e.g., 'мне холодно' = 'I feel cold').
The cardinal number equal to 40.
Used figuratively in older or literary texts to mean a large, indefinite number (as in set phrases).
Introduces a concession: introduces a fact that contrasts with or mitigates the main clause.
Marks a lower limit or minimal allowance: 'even, if only' (often in phrases like «хотя бы»).

The science and practice of diagnosing, treating, and preventing illness.
Medical care or therapy given to a patient.

A holder of a doctoral degree (PhD or equivalent).
To hold someone in high regard or esteem; to treat with deference.
To behave toward someone with politeness or ceremonious regard.
To observe or comply with (rules, agreements); to not violate.
(colloquial/lighter) To be fond of someone or something.
Indicates motion or orientation toward someone or something (dative).
Marks a time or limit ('by [a time]') or purpose/recipient in some contexts.
Used in contexts meaning 'against' (less common; e.g. legal or adversarial senses).
Dative singular form meaning 'to/for that' (masculine or neuter); used to indicate recipient or indirect object.
Dative form used where neuter demonstrative/particle 'то' functions as the referent ('to/for it/that').
Emphasises identity or intensity ('the very, the same, indeed').
Marks contrast or shift ('whereas, on the other hand, as for').
Connective particle sometimes rendered 'and' or 'but' in informal sequences.
With time expressions, emphasises promptness ('right away, immediately').
Prone to believe in superstitions; guided by irrational beliefs about luck or omens.
Marks the limit in time or extent ('until, up to').
Indicates a point earlier than in time or position ('before').
Used to mean 'with respect to' or 'concerning'.

Used to start or transition in speech, roughly 'well' or 'now'.

To have the ability or possibility to do something.
To succeed in doing something (often after effort).
To make excrement on something (literal, coarse).
To spoil or make a dirty/messy situation (figurative, coarse).

Having positive qualities; satisfactory or of good quality.
Kind-hearted or benevolent in character.
Competent or able in a particular activity (a good [worker, musician]).

Entire; comprising all parts of something.
All of a group or quantity (the whole of).

Solely; to the exclusion of others.
Used to limit or narrow a statement (just/merely).
First-person singular future of повредить; to cause physical harm or injury to something.
To harm or impair non-physical things (reputation, health, function).
Not any person; nobody.

Comparative indicating a greater quantity or degree.
Used with negation (больше не) to mean 'no longer' or 'not anymore'.
Indicating a larger amount, extent, or age (comparative sense).
All people; every person in a group.
All things or the whole of something (collective sense).

A particle adding emphasis that something happened or is true despite expectations.
Used to stress or contradict, similar to 'indeed' or 'still' in English.
Having bad moral qualities or character.
Of low quality, defective, or unsatisfactory.
Causing harm or disadvantage; dangerous or undesirable.
The quality of having a fine cutting or pointed edge.

To compose or finish a written work or piece of writing.
To move out of a place; to depart or step outside.
To appear publicly or be issued (of a book, play, or news).
To result or happen in a particular way.
Used in the phrase 'выйти замуж' meaning to marry (of a woman).
Used to a high degree; greatly or extremely.
In a manner that is physically sharp or piercing.
With strong, intense effect (of pain, feeling, or perception).
In a biting or incisive manner (of criticism, remark, or wit).
To perceive with the eyes; to catch sight of someone or something.
To come to perceive or understand something mentally.
Referring back to the subject to indicate the person acting alone or personally.
Indicating identity or exact sameness (the very same one).
Used to emphasize the subject or to mean 'indeed' or 'even'.
Indicating that someone is by themselves or acting without help.
In a manner that provokes strong disgust or loathing.
In a morally contemptible or odious way.
To behave ostentatiously to attract attention or appear impressive.
Intentionally; done on purpose rather than by accident.
With a specific aim or design; made or done for a particular reason.
Earlier in time; previously.
Especially; above all (as in 'прежде всего').

To perform duties or provide service for someone or something.
To function as or be used for a particular purpose.

A person holding a government or bureaucratic office; a civil servant.

Impolite or coarse in manner.
Having a coarse, unrefined, or harsh quality or texture.

To experience or feel (as in 'находить удовольствие' — to take pleasure).

Used to appeal to shared knowledge or to reinforce a statement.

To receive or accept something (payment, responsibility, offer).

The meal or table service (e.g., 'to sit at the table' = to dine); used figuratively for hospitality.
A working surface or desk, often in administrative contexts.

Genitive/accusative form used to mean 'of whom' or 'of which' depending on context and animacy.
To be seated on a chair or other support.
To remain in a place or state (e.g., to stay at home, to remain inactive).

One who begs or entreats for aid or favor, often in a humble or formal manner.
Indicates position at the back of or beyond something (location).
Indicates support, exchange, payment, or acting on someone's behalf.

A brief note or piece of information used for inquiry or reference.

At last; after a delay or sequence, marking the end or culmination.

To defeat or get the better of an obstacle, enemy, or difficulty.
To overpower someone emotionally or physically (e.g., fatigue, fear).

To be composed or made up of specified parts or members.

senses.gloss: to cut the legs from under
senses.definition: To cause someone or something to fall by literally or figuratively cutting support from beneath.

To strive or contend with someone or something in a non‑literal or competitive sense.

Thin, flat pieces or flakes (commonly of snow or similar light material).

To open or unfold (a door, book, flower, etc.).

Bleak, lonely, or barren in appearance or atmosphere.

A wary attitude or carefulness in action or speech.
Conforming to the current style or fashion; in vogue.

A person who has or affects refined sensitivity to beauty and the arts.
Relating to beauty or artistic taste (rare adjectival use, chiefly older/literary).
To come into possession of something; to obtain or gain.
To acquire a quality, reputation, habit, or condition over time.
Marked by irony; using words or situations that convey a meaning opposite to their literal sense.
Having a dry, mocking, or subtly contemptuous tone.
To choke or cough because something has caught in the throat while swallowing.
Figuratively, to be taken aback or falter in speech because of surprise or shock.
With firmness or resolute determination.
In a stable or solid manner; securely fixed.
In a definite, unhesitating way; certainly.
To be wrong in belief, judgment, or opinion.
To commit an error in action, calculation, or assessment.
In a servile, excessively submissive manner.
In the literal manner of a slave, as if enslaved or forced.
To enter into or discuss something at length (e.g., вдаваться в подробности — to go into details).
To be pressed or sunk into a surface or space; to become indented.
To give oneself over to an activity or state, to be drawn into something (often reflexive).
To feel fear or be frightened.
To react with sudden alarm or surprise; to startle.
To hesitate or recoil from doing something because of fear.
In a way that causes physical pain or acute distress.
In a way suggesting ill health, excessive sensitivity, or an unhealthy intensity.
Dative singular of 'глаз', meaning 'to/for the eye'.
Locative (prepositional) singular of 'глаз', an archaic/literary form meaning 'in/on the eye' or 'about the eye'.
Second-person plural (or polite singular) future indicative of 'отдать': 'you will give' or 'you will hand over'.
Used in questions as a polite or entreating formula: 'would you give / could you please give (me)...?'.
Plural past imperfective of 'слабеть': 'they/people grew weak' or 'lost strength'.
Plural past imperfective used of forces, feelings, or intensity: 'they abated' or 'they subsided'.
Worthless, broken, or discarded things; junk or rubbish.
Accumulation of useless household stuff or odds and ends; clutter (pejorative).
Lit by light or made bright by a light source.
Figuratively made clear or illuminated in thought or understanding.
Made known to the public or treated in the press (covered).
Instrumental plural of кий: with rods or sticks used for striking (e.g., billiard cues, clubs).
Instrumental plural used for long poles or club-like sticks in older or regional usage.
Serving to suggest or evoke the likeness of something; resembling.
Causing someone to remember something or bringing something to mind.
Passionate intensity or fervor of feeling or action (instrumental form: 'with ardor').
A quality of impulsive eagerness or fiery haste in behavior or speech.
To become old or elderly; to age (of a person).
To grow old together with someone or in company.
To become worn out, out of fashion, or no longer suitable (figurative).
To utter words or express information; to tell or state something.
To inform, reveal, or announce news, a secret, or an intention.
Attractive or pleasing in appearance (especially of a woman).
Convenient, appropriate, or well-suited for a purpose or occasion (often of things or times).
Second-person singular imperative: an exhortation or command to continue living or to reside.
Used imperatively or exhortatively to tell someone to continue as they are or to leave matters be (figurative).
To move slowly while dragging or trailing (often with feet dragging) behind someone or something.
To linger, dawdle, or move about aimlessly and slowly.
To follow or cling to someone persistently (figurative).
To wait standing until one's turn is reached (e.g., to stand in a queue).
To endure or stand through a period of hardship or difficulty.
To leave something to stand so it ripens or develops (e.g., food, drink).
Physically dirty or impure; unclean.
Morally corrupt, indecent, or obscene.
A supernatural unclean being or demon in folklore and poetry.
To make a fraudulent copy or counterfeit, especially of a document or signature.
To alter or tamper with something so as to misrepresent its authenticity.
To do a small paid job on the side (colloquial sense).
To become fastened with chains or shackles; to be physically bound.
To become fixed or riveted (of gaze, attention, or feeling) so that it cannot move away.
To become closely attached or fastened to something or someone (often of clothes or objects catching on something).
Produced by a printing process; reproduced on paper or similar material by type or press.
Issued or made public in print (of books, newspapers, pamphlets).
The act of executing oneself; causing one's own death as a formal punishment or extreme act.
Punishment inflicted on oneself, either literally or as a penitent act; used figuratively for severe self-reproach.
To write something down on a list or into a register.
To carry or bring something into a place.
